headline: Bank of America’s (NYSE:BAC) Q2 CY2026: Beats On Revenue
summary: Financial services giant Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) reported Q2 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 14.2% year on year to $31.56 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.21 per share was 7.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.summary: Financial services giant Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) reported Q2 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 14.2% year on year to $31.56 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.21 per share was 7.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Bank of America Earnings Top Wall Street Estimates

headline: ‘Extraordinary’ Earnings Results Lift Bank Stocks
summary: Bank stocks were rising Tuesday morning after the nation’s largest banks reported strong second-quarter earnings due to powerhouse results from investment banking units which have reaped the benefits of a surge in dealmaking activity.  “We thought the 2Q earnings were going to be very good, but they turned out to be extraordinary,” Macrae Sykes, portfolio manager of the GABF ETF at Gabelli Funds, said in a statement.  The KBW Bank Index was up 1.75%, while the benchmark S&P 500 index was up 0.28%.

content.title: ‘Extraordinary’ Earnings Results Lift Bank Stocks
content.summary: Bank stocks were rising Tuesday morning after the nation’s largest banks reported strong second-quarter earnings due to powerhouse results from investment banking units which have reaped the benefits of a surge in dealmaking activity.  “We thought the 2Q earnings were going to be very good, but they turned out to be extraordinary,” Macrae Sykes, portfolio manager of the GABF ETF at Gabelli Funds, said in a statement.  The KBW Bank Index was up 1.75%, while the benchmark S&P 500 index was up 0.28%.

headline: Buoyant Stock Market Boosts Wealth Units at Major Banks
summary: Wealth management units at the nation’s largest banks reported rising client assets for the second quarter, thanks to a combination of market appreciation and asset-gathering.  More money in client investment accounts is a boon for the fees that the wealth units generate on assets under management.  Citigroup reported client investment assets rose 14% year over year to $727 billion.

content.title: Buoyant Stock Market Boosts Wealth Units at Major Banks

More money in client investment accounts is a boon for the fees that the wealth units generate on assets under management.  Citigroup reported client investment assets rose 14% year over year to $727 billion.

headline: Consumer Credit: What Bank of America Results Tell Us
summary: The amount of money Bank of America sets aside in case of potentially bad loans remained relatively unchanged from the first quarter.  Bank of America said its companywide provision for credit losses stood at $1.366 billion for the second quarter compared with $1.337 billion for the first quarter.  For the second quarter of 2025, the bank’s provision for credit losses was $1.592 billion.

content.title: Consumer Credit: What Bank of America Results Tell Us
content.summary: The amount of money Bank of America sets aside in case of potentially bad loans remained relatively unchanged from the first quarter.  Bank of America said its companywide provision for credit losses stood at $1.366 billion for the second quarter compared with $1.337 billion for the first quarter.  For the second quarter of 2025, the bank’s provision for credit losses was $1.592 billion.
